stem: The formula for the velocity of an object is v = u + at, where v is the final velocity, u is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and t is the time. Which of the following correctly expresses the acceleration a in terms of the other variables?
explanation: Starting with v = u + at, subtract u from both sides to get v - u = at. Then divide both sides by t to isolate a, giving a = (v - u)/t.

distractorRationale.A: Student multiplied by t instead of dividing by t when isolating a from at = v - u
distractorRationale.B: Correct answer obtained by properly subtracting u and dividing by t
distractorRationale.C: Student subtracted t instead of dividing by t when trying to isolate a
distractorRationale.D: Student added u instead of subtracting u when rearranging the formula
